event: no history of hematuria or passing stones
peripheral blood smear showed toxic granulation of WBCs
retrospective measurement of calcium at 14 weeks’ gestation was obtained at 2.43 mmol/l
serum protein electrophoresis as well as serum level of angiotensin-converting enzyme were normal
given normal saline infusion at 250 ml/h
close monitoring of her urine output
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
possible diagnosis was humoral hypercalcemia of benignancy (uterine fibroid)
PTH-rP is the primary mediator that causes hypercalcemia
did not check for the PTH-rP level because of lack of facilities
nephrologist was consulted for urgent hemodialysis
delivery of a baby girl weighing 2.9 kg
removal of uterine masses measuring in aggregate 30 × 25 × 15 cm
histopathology report was consistent with leiomyoma with calcifications but no evidence of malignancy
postoperative clinical course was further complicated by septicemia and septic shock
CT scan of the chest and pelvis showed no evidence of mass lesion or organomegaly
MRI of the brain showed evidence of infarcts involving the right cerebellum
cortical regions in frontal, parietal, and occipital lobes
MRA revealed evidence of vasospasm of the internal carotid
the middle and the anterior carotid arteries
findings are suggestive of diffuse ischemic changes secondary to hypoxic insult
final diagnosis of humoral hypercalcemia associated with a uterine fibroid
Serum calcium remained within the normal range over the subsequent 6 months
